Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, indicating possible glomerular damage. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor) further support renal involvement. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation for possible chronic kidney disease and further assessment of proteinuria etiology.
